votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, matching all grade boundaries and list relations exactly.
The Python code correctly translates the Coq specification, accurately implementing the grade relation conditions, the precondition, and the Forall2 list matching logic.
The Python code correctly translates the Coq specifications, including the grade relation intervals, the precondition, and the Forall2 list matching logic.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. The grade_relation function checks the same conditions as the Coq grade_relation Prop (equality at 4.0 for A+, strict inequality 3.7 < gpa < 4.0 for A, and half-open intervals for other grades matching the Coq spec). The precondition checks all GPAs are in [0,4]. The spec uses Forall2 semantics correctly by checking equal lengths and pairwise grade_relation. The Python implementation will return the same boolean judgments as the Coq specification for all concrete inputs.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. The grade_relation function checks the same conditions as the Coq definition (with the same boundary conditions for each grade range). The problem_81_pre checks that all GPAs are in [0,4]. The problem_81_spec uses zip with length check to implement Forall2, verifying that corresponding elements satisfy grade_relation. All concrete inputs will produce the same results.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. The grade_relation function checks the same conditions as the Coq grade_relation Prop (exact equality for 4.0 and 0.0, and the same interval conditions for other grades). The precondition problem_81_pre checks that all GPAs are in [0,4]. The spec problem_81_spec uses Forall2 semantics by checking equal lengths and that grade_relation holds for all corresponding pairs. The Python implementation will return the same boolean judgments as the Coq specification for all concrete inputs.

Python mirrors the Coq intervals and endpoints exactly (including 4.0->A+, 0.0->E, and each half-open range), enforces the same precondition bounds, and matches Forall2 via length check and pairwise grade_relation.
Python matches the Coq relation intervals and boundaries, the precondition 0<=g<=4, and the Forall2 pairing via equal lengths and per-element checks.
Interval thresholds and inclusivity match exactly; precondition and pairwise relation (Forall2) are implemented equivalently with length check and per-element grade_relation.
